# Job 22

## Verses (30)

| # | Basic English Bible | Compare |
|---|---|---|
| [1](BBE.md#v1) | Then Eliphaz the Temanite made answer and said, | [WEB](WEB.md#v1) · [KJV](KJV.md#v1) · [ASV](ASV.md#v1) |
| [2](BBE.md#v2) | Is it possible for a man to be of profit to God? No, for a man's wisdom is only of profit to himself. | [WEB](WEB.md#v2) · [KJV](KJV.md#v2) · [ASV](ASV.md#v2) |
| [3](BBE.md#v3) | Is it of any interest to the Ruler of all that you are upright? or is it of use to him that your ways are without sin? | [WEB](WEB.md#v3) · [KJV](KJV.md#v3) · [ASV](ASV.md#v3) |
| [4](BBE.md#v4) | Is it because you give him honour that he is sending punishment on you and is judging you? | [WEB](WEB.md#v4) · [KJV](KJV.md#v4) · [ASV](ASV.md#v4) |
| [5](BBE.md#v5) | Is not your evil-doing great? and there is no end to your sins. | [WEB](WEB.md#v5) · [KJV](KJV.md#v5) · [ASV](ASV.md#v5) |
| [6](BBE.md#v6) | For you have taken your brother's goods when he was not in your debt, and have taken away the clothing of those who have need of it. | [WEB](WEB.md#v6) · [KJV](KJV.md#v6) · [ASV](ASV.md#v6) |
| [7](BBE.md#v7) | You do not give water to the tired traveller, and from him who has no food you keep back bread. | [WEB](WEB.md#v7) · [KJV](KJV.md#v7) · [ASV](ASV.md#v7) |
| [8](BBE.md#v8) | For it was the man with power who had the land, and the man with an honoured name who was living in it. | [WEB](WEB.md#v8) · [KJV](KJV.md#v8) · [ASV](ASV.md#v8) |
| [9](BBE.md#v9) | You have sent widows away without hearing their cause, and you have taken away the support of the child who has no father. | [WEB](WEB.md#v9) · [KJV](KJV.md#v9) · [ASV](ASV.md#v9) |
| [10](BBE.md#v10) | For this cause nets are round your feet, and you are overcome with sudden fear. | [WEB](WEB.md#v10) · [KJV](KJV.md#v10) · [ASV](ASV.md#v10) |
| [11](BBE.md#v11) | Your light is made dark so that you are unable to see, and you are covered by a mass of waters. | [WEB](WEB.md#v11) · [KJV](KJV.md#v11) · [ASV](ASV.md#v11) |
| [12](BBE.md#v12) | Is not God as high as heaven? and see the stars, how high they are! | [WEB](WEB.md#v12) · [KJV](KJV.md#v12) · [ASV](ASV.md#v12) |
| [13](BBE.md#v13) | And you say, What knowledge has God? is he able to give decisions through the deep dark? | [WEB](WEB.md#v13) · [KJV](KJV.md#v13) · [ASV](ASV.md#v13) |
| [14](BBE.md#v14) | Thick clouds are covering him, so that he is unable to see; and he is walking on the arch of heaven. | [WEB](WEB.md#v14) · [KJV](KJV.md#v14) · [ASV](ASV.md#v14) |
| [15](BBE.md#v15) | Will you keep the old way by which evil men went? | [WEB](WEB.md#v15) · [KJV](KJV.md#v15) · [ASV](ASV.md#v15) |
| [16](BBE.md#v16) | Who were violently taken away before their time, who were overcome by the rush of waters: | [WEB](WEB.md#v16) · [KJV](KJV.md#v16) · [ASV](ASV.md#v16) |
| [17](BBE.md#v17) | Who said to God, Go away from us; and, What is the Ruler of all able to do to us? | [WEB](WEB.md#v17) · [KJV](KJV.md#v17) · [ASV](ASV.md#v17) |
| [18](BBE.md#v18) | Though he made their houses full of good things: but the purpose of the evil-doers is far from me! | [WEB](WEB.md#v18) · [KJV](KJV.md#v18) · [ASV](ASV.md#v18) |
| [19](BBE.md#v19) | The upright saw it and were glad: and those who had done no wrong made sport of them, | [WEB](WEB.md#v19) · [KJV](KJV.md#v19) · [ASV](ASV.md#v19) |
| [20](BBE.md#v20) | Saying, Truly, their substance is cut off, and their wealth is food for the fire. | [WEB](WEB.md#v20) · [KJV](KJV.md#v20) · [ASV](ASV.md#v20) |
| [21](BBE.md#v21) | Put yourself now in a right relation with him and be at peace: so will you do well in your undertakings. | [WEB](WEB.md#v21) · [KJV](KJV.md#v21) · [ASV](ASV.md#v21) |
| [22](BBE.md#v22) | Be pleased to take teaching from his mouth, and let his words be stored up in your heart. | [WEB](WEB.md#v22) · [KJV](KJV.md#v22) · [ASV](ASV.md#v22) |
| [23](BBE.md#v23) | If you come back to the Ruler of all, making yourself low before him; if you put evil far away from your tents; | [WEB](WEB.md#v23) · [KJV](KJV.md#v23) · [ASV](ASV.md#v23) |
| [24](BBE.md#v24) | And put your gold in the dust, even your gold of Ophir among the rocks of the valleys; | [WEB](WEB.md#v24) · [KJV](KJV.md#v24) · [ASV](ASV.md#v24) |
| [25](BBE.md#v25) | Then the Ruler of all will be your gold, and his teaching will be your silver; | [WEB](WEB.md#v25) · [KJV](KJV.md#v25) · [ASV](ASV.md#v25) |
| [26](BBE.md#v26) | For then you will have delight in the Ruler of all, and your face will be lifted up to God. | [WEB](WEB.md#v26) · [KJV](KJV.md#v26) · [ASV](ASV.md#v26) |
| [27](BBE.md#v27) | You will make your prayer to him, and be answered; and you will give effect to your oaths. | [WEB](WEB.md#v27) · [KJV](KJV.md#v27) · [ASV](ASV.md#v27) |
| [28](BBE.md#v28) | Your purposes will come about, and light will be shining on your ways. | [WEB](WEB.md#v28) · [KJV](KJV.md#v28) · [ASV](ASV.md#v28) |
| [29](BBE.md#v29) | For God makes low those whose hearts are lifted up, but he is a saviour to the poor in spirit. | [WEB](WEB.md#v29) · [KJV](KJV.md#v29) · [ASV](ASV.md#v29) |
| [30](BBE.md#v30) | He makes safe the man who is free from sin, and if your hands are clean, salvation will be yours. | [WEB](WEB.md#v30) · [KJV](KJV.md#v30) · [ASV](ASV.md#v30) |
